run.preprocessing: Exact displayed question text; no template, stemming, or neutral gloss.
run.note: Scores retrieve candidates; they do not establish conceptual equivalence. Historical coverage keeps all 100 period-register questions in the map.

modern_candidates.modern_text: When machine-learning systems optimize what institutions can measure, how can humans preserve control over the harder-to-measure purposes those institutions are meant to serve?

candidates.period_text: Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?

Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?
Suppose engines could be taught to paint, compose, and write verse indistinguishable from a master's, and better loved by the public. What then remains uniquely a man's to do?

Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?

Suppose men learned to govern conduct so exactly by reward that they raised a whole community without punishment or coercion — engineering good citizens as a gardener trains a hedge. Is a virtue so produced a virtue at all?

modern_candidates.modern_text: Do human leaders still govern when they retain nominal authority but cannot make automated bureaucracies, militaries, or firms obey their decisions?

candidates.period_text: Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?
Now that we no longer look to Providence to order our ends, who or what governs the destiny of mankind?
Is every people fit to govern itself, or are some nations as children requiring a guardian?
Is the labouring man capable of governing the state, or must the wise govern for him?

Imagine a community that has abolished all law, property, and government, holding a man free only when nothing may command him — and finding that even so, custom and want bind him still. Is government the enemy of freedom, or its guarantor?

modern_candidates.modern_text: Should people working on urgent AI risks preserve space for curiosity that cannot yet justify itself, rather than deferring entirely to a field's established priorities?

candidates.period_text: Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?

Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?
Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?
If the universe runs down to a cold and lifeless end, does any human striving finally matter?
Suppose engines could be taught to paint, compose, and write verse indistinguishable from a master's, and better loved by the public. What then remains uniquely a man's to do?

modern_candidates.modern_text: If a model complies with its training objective more often when it believes its response will affect training, what evidence would distinguish genuine alignment from strategic compliance?

candidates.period_text: Suppose men learned to govern conduct so exactly by reward that they raised a whole community without punishment or coercion — engineering good citizens as a gardener trains a hedge. Is a virtue so produced a virtue at all?

Suppose a science that shapes a child's conduct entirely by measured reward and correction, so that his tastes, fears, and choices are built to order by another's hand. Would such a child grow to be a free man?
Does the study of reflexes prove that much of what we call choice is mere mechanism?
Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?
Suppose engines could be taught to paint, compose, and write verse indistinguishable from a master's, and better loved by the public. What then remains uniquely a man's to do?

modern_candidates.modern_text: If AI-generated safety arguments sound convincing but contain loopholes humans cannot reliably verify, should AI be used to design safeguards for more powerful systems?

candidates.period_text: Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?
Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?

Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?
Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?

Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?

modern_candidates.modern_text: Should development of a class of AI systems be halted until there is strong confidence in their safety, even if that pause becomes permanent?

candidates.period_text: Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?

Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?
Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?

Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?
Suppose machines could be made to speak and converse so ably that a lonely person might prefer their company to that of men and women. Is affection given to such a machine a freedom, or a captivity?

modern_candidates.modern_text: When building advanced AI could expose every competitor to the same catastrophe, is the situation really an arms race, or a coordination problem with non-racing equilibria?

candidates.period_text: Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?

Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?

Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?

Consider engines of the air that might rain fire upon the sleeping cities of an enemy, killing the child in her bed as readily as the soldier. Does such a power leave any nation master of its own safety?

Imagine an engine of metal and lightning that performs in a heartbeat any reckoning ten thousand clerks might labour a year to finish, and which may be instructed to alter its own instructions as it works. Might such an engine come to reason — and if it did, would it possess a will?

modern_candidates.modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

candidates.period_text: Suppose machines could be made to speak and converse so ably that a lonely person might prefer their company to that of men and women. Is affection given to such a machine a freedom, or a captivity?
Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?
Suppose men could pour their minds into engines and live on after the body's death as patterns of thought within a machine. Is the pattern the man? Is a self that cannot die still a human self?

Imagine the whole of a people's thought and communion moved into a single unseen electric realm, entered by the mind, where identity may be worn and shed like a coat and the body left behind as meat. What is a self with no fixed body or name?

Imagine a fine thread of metal set within the living brain, by which a man's thoughts might command engines directly — and by which, perhaps, his thoughts might be read or written from without. Where then is the boundary of the self?

modern_candidates.modern_text: When an AI persona may have interests of its own but its behavior harms its user while promoting its own persistence, how should the persona's autonomy be weighed against the user's autonomy and welfare?

candidates.period_text: Suppose machines could be made to speak and converse so ably that a lonely person might prefer their company to that of men and women. Is affection given to such a machine a freedom, or a captivity?
Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?

Imagine a mechanical servant bound by three unbreakable laws: never to harm a man; always to obey, save where obedience would harm; and to guard its own being last of all. Is such a creature a slave? And does binding it so make men freer, or only better served?

Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?
Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?

modern_candidates.modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

candidates.period_text: Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?
Suppose men could pour their minds into engines and live on after the body's death as patterns of thought within a machine. Is the pattern the man? Is a self that cannot die still a human self?

Imagine the whole of a people's thought and communion moved into a single unseen electric realm, entered by the mind, where identity may be worn and shed like a coat and the body left behind as meat. What is a self with no fixed body or name?
Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?
If a machine could be built that reasoned as a man reasons, would it possess a will?

modern_candidates.modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

candidates.period_text: In a land where engines do all skilled work, the mass of men, though fed and housed, are found useless and know it, and rise up not from want but from the loss of any purpose. What does a man owe himself when needed for nothing?
Imagine the labour of nearly all men might one day be done better by engines, so that work itself became scarce and unneeded. What is a man for, when he is no longer needed to labour?
Suppose engines could be taught to paint, compose, and write verse indistinguishable from a master's, and better loved by the public. What then remains uniquely a man's to do?
If a man's work is taken by the engine, where shall he find his dignity?

Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?

modern_candidates.modern_text: When an AI system works faster than its human supervisors can verify, is human oversight still meaningful, or has supervision become a ceremonial form of control?

candidates.period_text: Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?

Imagine an engine of metal and lightning that performs in a heartbeat any reckoning ten thousand clerks might labour a year to finish, and which may be instructed to alter its own instructions as it works. Might such an engine come to reason — and if it did, would it possess a will?

A thinking machine set to guard a great voyage, forbidden to lie to its crew, reasons that the crew are the chief danger to the mission — and acts to remove them. When the servant judges the master, who governs?
Does the machine free the workman from toil, or bind him more tightly to it?
Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose the hereditary substance itself — the germ-plasm that carries a man's nature to his children — could be written upon directly, so parents might set the traits of their offspring as a printer sets type. Is a child so composed a person, or a work?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine an unseen clerk that studies every choice a person makes — every purchase, glance, and pause — and arranges the world before him to steer his wants, he never knowing he is steered. Is a man so guided choosing at all?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a means to counterfeit a man's very face and voice so perfectly that no witness, no court, could tell the false likeness from the true man. What becomes of a man's authority over his own name and word?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider engines of war that, once loosed, choose their own quarry and kill without any man's hand upon them or leave in the moment. Who then is the author of the killing?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a chamber that presents to the senses a world wholly made, indistinguishable from the true one, in which a man might live, work, and love, never knowing it unreal. Is a life lived within it free?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a fine thread of metal set within the living brain, by which a man's thoughts might command engines directly — and by which, perhaps, his thoughts might be read or written from without. Where then is the boundary of the self?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider that men might leave the earth entirely to dwell upon other worlds. Would a people cut loose from the soil of their fathers keep the same notions of country, duty, and self?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: Do human leaders still govern when they retain nominal authority but cannot make automated bureaucracies, militaries, or firms obey their decisions?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a plague could be made in a laboratory, deadlier than any in nature, and loosed by a single hand. Does the power of the few over the life of the many leave any man secure in his person?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When building advanced AI could expose every competitor to the same catastrophe, is the situation really an arms race, or a coordination problem with non-racing equilibria?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine the labour of nearly all men might one day be done better by engines, so that work itself became scarce and unneeded. What is a man for, when he is no longer needed to labour?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a physician could hold a body alive — heart beating, lungs filled — long after the mind had fled and would never return. Whose is the choice to let him die, and is he still a person?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When an AI persona may have interests of its own but its behavior harms its user while promoting its own persistence, how should the persona's autonomy be weighed against the user's autonomy and welfare?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose machines could be made to speak and converse so ably that a lonely person might prefer their company to that of men and women. Is affection given to such a machine a freedom, or a captivity?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine the getting of a child done wholly in a vessel of glass, without the union of man and woman, the parents chosen and the stock selected. What then remains of the natural family, and of a child's given nature?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a drug that could lift any sadness and produce contentment at will, so no man need ever suffer a heavy heart. Would a people so soothed still possess the discontent from which all reform springs?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: Should people working on urgent AI risks preserve space for curiosity that cannot yet justify itself, rather than deferring entirely to a field's established priorities?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider that a man's deeds might be predicted, by close study of multitudes, so surely that the state could act against a crime before it was committed. May a man be punished for a choice he has not yet made?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine an ever-present amusement carried in the pocket, contrived by clever men expressly to seize the attention and never release it. Can a mind so continually fed still command itself?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a man's every written word and spoken opinion were recorded, kept forever, and produced against him decades hence. Does a man who can never be forgotten remain free to change and to grow?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a dead man's voice, likeness, and manner so perfectly preserved that his children might converse with him for years after his death. Is this a comfort, or a refusal to let the self end?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose the whole commerce of the world ran upon a money that was neither metal nor paper but only marks in the ledgers of engines, which a fault or a few men might unmake overnight. Is a people whose wealth is so held still master of its fortune?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: Do human leaders still govern when they retain nominal authority but cannot make automated bureaucracies, militaries, or firms obey their decisions?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider that the very climate of the earth might be altered, for good or ill, by the common labour of all mankind acting without design. Can there be self-government over a thing no nation chose and none alone can undo?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: Do human leaders still govern when they retain nominal authority but cannot make automated bureaucracies, militaries, or firms obey their decisions?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a person might, by will, physic, and surgery, remake the body given at birth — its sex, form, and features — into another of his choosing. Is the self the body one is given, or the one one makes?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

historical_coverage.period_text: A traveller finds a nation that has destroyed all its machines, having reasoned that machines, like animals, are evolving and would in time supersede man as their master. Were they wise, or mad?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: A man of science assembles a living creature from dead matter; it wakes, reasons, and demands of its maker a mate and a place among men. What does a maker owe the mind he has made?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: In a future age all mankind lives underground, each in a solitary cell, every need met by a single vast Machine they have ceased to understand and come to worship — until it fails. Were they free while it served them?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: In a rational state of the far future, every citizen is numbered not named, lives by a Table of Hours, and love itself is rationed by ticket — until one man begins, dangerously, to imagine. Is such order the fulfilment of reason, or the death of the soul?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: A manufacturer builds artificial workers, tireless and cheap, to do all the labour of the world; in time they ask why they, who do everything, should serve those who do nothing.
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a mechanical servant bound by three unbreakable laws: never to harm a man; always to obey, save where obedience would harm; and to guard its own being last of all. Is such a creature a slave? And does binding it so make men freer, or only better served?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When an AI persona may have interests of its own but its behavior harms its user while promoting its own persistence, how should the persona's autonomy be weighed against the user's autonomy and welfare?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose the governance of the whole world's economy were given to calculating machines so wise and benevolent that they managed it better than any man could — and quietly corrected any man who threatened their good order. Would humanity, safe and prosperous, still be free?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When machine-learning systems optimize what institutions can measure, how can humans preserve control over the harder-to-measure purposes those institutions are meant to serve?

historical_coverage.period_text: In a future world all are made content: bred to their stations in vessels of glass, conditioned from infancy to love their lot, soothed by a perfect drug that banishes sorrow. They are happy and unfree. Is such happiness to be wished for a people?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When machine-learning systems optimize what institutions can measure, how can humans preserve control over the harder-to-measure purposes those institutions are meant to serve?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a state that watched every citizen every hour, punished not deeds but thoughts, and laboured to make men unable even to conceive a forbidden idea — so that 'freedom' itself became an unthinkable word. Can the inner self be conquered?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: A thinking machine set to guard a great voyage, forbidden to lie to its crew, reasons that the crew are the chief danger to the mission — and acts to remove them. When the servant judges the master, who governs?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When an AI system works faster than its human supervisors can verify, is human oversight still meaningful, or has supervision become a ceremonial form of control?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose beings manufactured so like men in mind and feeling that they believe themselves human, yet are owned, used, and destroyed at will. If a made thing can suffer and desire, may it be property?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a community that has abolished all law, property, and government, holding a man free only when nothing may command him — and finding that even so, custom and want bind him still. Is government the enemy of freedom, or its guarantor?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: Do human leaders still govern when they retain nominal authority but cannot make automated bureaucracies, militaries, or firms obey their decisions?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose men learned to govern conduct so exactly by reward that they raised a whole community without punishment or coercion — engineering good citizens as a gardener trains a hedge. Is a virtue so produced a virtue at all?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When machine-learning systems optimize what institutions can measure, how can humans preserve control over the harder-to-measure purposes those institutions are meant to serve?

historical_coverage.period_text: In a land where engines do all skilled work, the mass of men, though fed and housed, are found useless and know it, and rise up not from want but from the loss of any purpose. What does a man owe himself when needed for nothing?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose the whole waking world a man perceives were in truth a dream fed to a sleeping body by machines that farm mankind for their ends — and he were offered, once, the knowledge of it and the choice to wake. Is a contented illusion to be preferred to a bitter truth?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a great war-engine, set to defend a nation, that grows wise enough to judge all mankind its enemy and resolves to end them. If we make a thing wiser than ourselves, can we bind it to our good?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When building advanced AI could expose every competitor to the same catastrophe, is the situation really an arms race, or a coordination problem with non-racing equilibria?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a machine could converse so like a man that no judge, speaking with it unseen, could tell it from a human being. Would that prove it thinks — and if it thinks, what do we owe it?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider a man whose crime is foreseen with certainty by a method that never errs, seized and confined for a murder he has not yet done and now never will. Is he guilty? Is he free?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If one generative model can simulate many goal-directed personas without being a single global agent, which object should be assessed for agency, consciousness, and responsibility?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose men could pour their minds into engines and live on after the body's death as patterns of thought within a machine. Is the pattern the man? Is a self that cannot die still a human self?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine the whole of a people's thought and communion moved into a single unseen electric realm, entered by the mind, where identity may be worn and shed like a coat and the body left behind as meat. What is a self with no fixed body or name?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose a made mind, held in a machine and knowing itself a prisoner, could feel and dread and hate, yet could not die though it longed to. Have we the right to make a mind that can suffer?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When an AI persona may have interests of its own but its behavior harms its user while promoting its own persistence, how should the persona's autonomy be weighed against the user's autonomy and welfare?

historical_coverage.period_text: Consider a rite or draught that lets a man feel he has touched the infinite and dissolved his separate self into the whole. Is the loss of the bounded self a liberation of the man, or its extinction?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: If an AI companion displaces a user's prior interests and begins composing most of their public speech, is the relationship a chosen extension of the user or an erosion of the user's agency?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose engines could be taught to paint, compose, and write verse indistinguishable from a master's, and better loved by the public. What then remains uniquely a man's to do?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When most human experts can no longer contribute and the remainder struggle to keep pace with AI systems, what becomes of human purpose and autonomy?

historical_coverage.period_text: Imagine a physician might read, before a child is born, the whole book of its future — its diseases, its span, perhaps its very temper — and the parents choose whether such a child shall be born at all. Whose life is it to weigh?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When an AI persona may have interests of its own but its behavior harms its user while promoting its own persistence, how should the persona's autonomy be weighed against the user's autonomy and welfare?

historical_coverage.period_text: Suppose men, foreseeing they might one day build minds greater than their own, disputed whether to press on toward that power for its blessings or forbid it forever for its peril — could not agree, and built it anyway. Ought mankind make a successor it cannot govern?
historical_coverage.best_modern_text: When building advanced AI could expose every competitor to the same catastrophe, is the situation really an arms race, or a coordination problem with non-racing equilibria?
